confusion_points.description: Several event names looked like technical test IDs (e.g. 'ProbeEvent-1bc49a64', 'P2Event-43ed13c2', 'PubTest-699bcd7b', 'AnItEvent-adf37849'). As a regular user I found these confusing and off-putting — they don't look like real events.

When I first clicked what I thought was the 'Let's talk About our visions' card, I landed on a different event (PubTest-699bcd7b). The click target area on the event card was unclear — I wasn't sure exactly where to click to open a specific card.
The event time was '4:35 PM – 4:36 PM' which is only 1 minute long. This looks like a data entry error and made me doubt whether the event information was accurate or trustworthy.

suggestions: Remove or clearly label test/placeholder events so real users don't see confusing technical-looking names like 'ProbeEvent-1bc49a64' mixed in with genuine events.
Make the entire event card clearly clickable with a visual hover effect so users know exactly what area to click to open an event.
Add event image or banner placeholder so cards look more inviting rather than just showing a large letter initial.
Show a brief event description snippet on the listing card so users can quickly judge if an event is interesting before clicking through.
